why_now_summary: You have several core interview mediums still at one-solve recall, and two common patterns—monotonic stack and BST validation—are still missing.
problem_reasons: Only solved once, and this hash-set pattern is a classic that gets fuzzy fast without a second clean pass.

primary_action: Do all 4 in order. Solve the first two fully from memory, then add short notes on the key invariant or decision rule you used.
secondary_action: If time is tight, complete the first 3 today and carry Validate Binary Search Tree into the next session.
stretch_action: After solving, spend 5 minutes comparing Course Schedule DFS vs BFS-toposort and Validate BST bounds-checking vs inorder approaches.

reasoning.summary: Refresh two core mediums that are still weakly consolidated, then close two important interview pattern gaps with manageable new problems.
reasoning.detailed: This session stays compact but high leverage. Longest Consecutive Sequence and Course Schedule are both important interview anchors that you've only solved once, which means recall is likely still shallow even though they are familiar. Daily Temperatures adds a missing monotonic-stack pattern in a very standard form, and Validate Binary Search Tree fills a common tree-invariant gap that shows up often in interviews. Together, this gives you breadth without diluting the session.

insights: Your medium coverage is broad, but many of the most interview-relevant ones are still single-solve only.
Graph and hash-set recall deserve reinforcement before they drift further.
Monotonic stack and BST validation are two of the clearest remaining core-pattern gaps.
